logistics.explanation: Bib collection occurs at Mirador Hotel Riu Palace Maspalomas on February 6 from 12:00pm to 9:00pm, with centralized start and finish at the same hotel. Broom bus support handles eliminations at Ayagaurers Viewpoint and Salto del Perro checkpoints. Multi-day schedule includes social ride and Riu Party for seamless participant flow.

length.explanation: Two-stage format totals around 100 miles with Stage 1 at 111 km (69 miles) and Stage 2 at 75 km (47 miles), both starting from Hotel Riu Palace Maspalomas. Distances suit gran fondo style without ultra-endurance demands. Time trials on climbs add segmented challenge to the overall route.

technicality.explanation: Paved roads on open traffic routes emphasize endurance over bike handling skills. No gravel or singletrack; focus remains on steady road climbing. Descents require caution due to public roads but lack high-speed technical sections.

elevation.explanation: Approximately 7500 feet of gain across volcanic island terrain with timed climbs like Ayagaurers Viewpoint and Salto del Perro. Stage 1 features two major time trial climbs with 45-minute and 1:50-hour cutoffs. Significant vertical tests pre-season fitness on Gran Canaria's rugged profile.

climate.explanation: February eternal spring weather on Gran Canaria offers mild temperatures ideal for early-season riding. Minimal rain risk enhances comfort during 8:30am starts from Maspalomas. Consistent conditions make it a reliable pre-season opener.

altitude.explanation: Low island elevations keep hypoxia effects negligible, with climbs topping out below 2000 meters. No sustained high-altitude exposure on routes from Maspalomas. Focus stays on cumulative gain rather than thin air challenges.

adventure.explanation: Volcanic island terrain delivers unique landscapes from Maspalomas through Ayagaurers and Salto del Perro. Gran Fondo World Tour opener adds global appeal with pro cyclist presence. Multi-stage format with parties elevates beyond standard sportive.

prestige.explanation: Gran Fondo World Tour season opener draws international field to Gran Canaria. UCI Class CPTR listing boosts credibility for amateur road event. Features world champions and Vuelta a España winners at Riu Party Club.

race_quality.explanation: Organized by Procycle Sport Club with Island Council of Gran Canaria support and Riu Hotels collaboration. Clear regulations include elimination checkpoints and broom bus at Ayagaurers Viewpoint. Two gran fondo stages with time trials ensure structured regional execution.

experience.explanation: Stunning volcanic routes from Hotel Riu Palace Maspalomas showcase Gran Canaria's diverse terrain. Social ride, Golden Hour, and Riu Party enhance weekend vibe. Time-trial climbs provide personal achievement highlights.

community.explanation: Around 2500 participants create vibrant atmosphere with national and international cyclists. Events like Friday social ride and Riu Party Club foster connections. Ties to local Canarian cycling culture via Procycle and federations.

field_depth.explanation: Capacity of 2500 riders matches typical 1000-5000 field for gran fondos. Past editions sold out in 48 hours to 500 early slots. Mix of amateurs and pros like Vuelta winners adds quality without elite dominance.

value.explanation: Multi-day event with two stages, social activities, and UCI recognition offers strong package. Pre-season timing leverages Gran Canaria's appeal without peak costs. Insurance via day license or RFEC enhances accessibility.

expenses.explanation: Island travel to Las Palmas incurs flights and hotel stays at Riu Palace Maspalomas area. Entry fees competitive but capacity limits drive quick sellouts. Local lodging and rentals balance moderate costs for international draw.

cultural_impact.explanation: Boosts Gran Canaria's sports tourism via Cabildo Insular collaborations. Regional highlight as Canary Islands' top road event since 2013. Attracts global riders but remains niche within Spanish cycling scene.

course_description.summary: Epic Gran Canaria spans two gran fondo stages over February 6-8 from Hotel Riu Palace Maspalomas, totaling approximately 100 miles on paved volcanic roads. Stage 1 covers 111 km with two timed climbs, while Stage 2 is 75 km featuring another time trial. Challenging elevation and island scenery mark it as a pre-season test.

key_segments.name: Ayagaurers Viewpoint Climb
key_segments.description: First elimination checkpoint time trial from Aqualand-Mirador with 45-minute cutoff. Steep paved ascent tests early pacing on Stage 1.

key_segments.name: Salto del Perro Climb
key_segments.description: Second Stage 1 time trial from Pasito Blanco with 1:50-hour limit, offering panoramic volcanic views. Demanding gradient near Maspalomas base.

key_segments.name: Stage 2 Time Trial
key_segments.description: Final climb summit timing on 75 km route from Hotel Riu Palace Maspalomas. Caps the weekend with shorter but punchy effort.

course_description.elevation_profile: Profile features multiple sharp climbs totaling 7500 feet, concentrated in time-trial segments like Ayagaurers and Salto del Perro. Volcanic terrain delivers relentless ups without long flats. Stages build from longer Stage 1 to punchier Stage 2 recovery.
course_description.aid_stations: Aid stations positioned at key points including elimination checkpoints. Organization provides support per RFEC gran fondo standards on open roads.

travel.driving_from_major_city: 20 miles, 30 minutes from Las Palmas de Gran Canaria
travel.accommodation: Riu Palace Maspalomas serves as race HQ with nearby hotels; book early for February peak.
travel.bike_transport: Rentals available locally; fly direct to LPA with bike bags or ship via standard services.

race_day.cutoff: Elimination at 45 min (Ayagaurers) and 1:50 hr (Salto del Perro) on Stage 1
race_day.categories: Age groups via RFEC license, two-stage gran fondo distances
race_day.awards: Time trial rankings, overall classifications by category

training_recommendations.minimum_fitness: Base of 2000 miles annual volume with weekly climbs to handle 7500 feet over two days.
training_recommendations.ideal_preparation: Build 8-12 weeks with back-to-back long rides mimicking 69+47 mile stages. Include 2-3 hour efforts at 200-300 watts with 3000+ feet per ride. Focus on time-trial pacing for Ayagaurers-style ascents.
training_recommendations.key_workouts: 3x20 min climbs at threshold targeting Salto del Perro gradients. 4-hour Saturday ride with 4000 feet followed by 2-hour Sunday spin. 90-min tempo intervals on rolling terrain.

history.notable_facts: Evolved into Gran Fondo World Tour opener with UCI CPTR status. Past editions sold out in 48 hours. Attracts pros like Vuelta a España stage winners for prestige.

final_verdict.one_liner: Pre-season volcanic punchbowl opener that kicks off GFWT with island flair.
final_verdict.who_should_ride: Ambitious amateurs seeking early-year challenge with 2500-rider energy. Climbers eyeing time trials on Ayagaurers and Salto del Perro.
final_verdict.who_should_skip: Flatland sprinters or those unready for 7500 feet in February chill. Beginners lacking gran fondo base fitness.
final_verdict.insider_tip: Arrive Thursday for presentation at Maspalomas to snag early bib and scout social ride route.

suffering_zones.label: Ayagaurers Viewpoint Grind
suffering_zones.description: 45-minute cutoff forces relentless pacing up the initial Stage 1 climb from Aqualand. Exposed volcanic slopes amplify early fatigue under Canarian sun.

suffering_zones.label: Salto del Perro Inferno
suffering_zones.description: 1:50-hour limit on the longest time trial demands sustained power after 50+ miles. Steep pitches near Pasito Blanco break legs before Maspalomas return.

suffering_zones.label: Stage 2 Accumulator
suffering_zones.description: Post-recovery climb time trial compounds weekend fatigue on 75 km loop. Shorter distance hides the cumulative toll of volcanic repeats.
